rulings.Case Title: CLINTON CHOY INDIVIDUALLY ET AL VS. DC DESIGN & CONSTRUCTION SERVICES, INC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, June 12, 2015, Line 7, DEFENDANTS DC DESIGN & CONSTRUCTION SERVICES, INC, DAVID CHANG, AND PAOLIN CHANG'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled as the alter ego allegations, the first cause of action for breach of contract, the third cause of action for negligent misrepresentation and the fourth cause of cause of action for intentional misrepresentation as to David Chang and DC Design Construction Services and sustained without leave to amend as to the second cause of action for negligence and the fourth cause of action for intentional misrepresentation as to Paolin Chang. Defendants have twenty days to file an answer. The alter ego allegations are adequately pled. (See, e.g., Rutherford Holdings, LLC v. Plaza Del Rey (2014) 223 Cal. App. 4th 221, 235-36). Defendants' arguments that the breach of contract claim is barred due to the administrative proceeding were previously rejected in the prior demurrer and must be re-raised, if at all, by a motion other than a demurrer. The contract attached to the first amended complaint is deemed attached to the second amended complaint and, so deemed, the breach of contract claim is adequately alleged. The second amended complaint did not cure the defect identified in the prior demurrer order as to the negligence claim (i.e. the negligence claim still is not alleged in a way that is distinct from breach of contract claim) and, thus having been given one opportunity to correct the defect, plaintiff clearly is unable to state a viable negligence claim. The misrepresentation claims are adequately pled except the intentional misrepresentation claim did not cure the defect identified in the prior demurrer order as to Paolin Chang (i.e. alleging how, where, to whom and by what means the alleged misrepresentations were made by Paolin Chang) and, thus, having been given an opportunity to correct the defect, plaintiff clearly is unable to state a viable intentional misrepresentation claim against Paolin Chang. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: ANNE H LEE VS. SHACUAN SHARON SETO AKA SHARON SETO et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, June 12, 2015, Line 9, DEFENDANTS SHACUAN SHARON SETO AKA SHARON SETO, DAVID SETO, GORDON LEE, RAISSI REAL ESTATE DEVELOPMENT, LLC, WINCHESTER PLAZA ON THE ROWE LLC, AND GREENLIGHT CAPITAL GROUP, LLC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled with twenty days to file an answer. The new allegations at 3:20-4:3 of the amended complaint allege the fraudulent misrepresentations with the requisite specificity and the defendants' scienter. Paragraph 5 of the amended complaint alleges that the defendants were agents/co-conspirators and those allegations support liability against each defendant.( Applied Equipment Corp. v. Litton Saudi Arabia Ltd. (1994) 7 Cal.4th 503, 510-511 ("Conspiracy is not a cause of action, but a legal doctrine that imposes liability on persons who, although not actually committing a tort themselves, share with the immediate tortfeasors a common plan or design in its perpetration¿By participation in a civil conspiracy, a coconspirator effectively adopts as his or her own the torts of other coconspirators within the ambit of the conspiracy. In this way, a coconspirator incurs tort liability co-equal with the immediate tortfeasors.") ) The concealment cause of action by its very nature needs to be alleged with less particularity. Less particularity is required where the defendant must necessarily possess full information concerning the facts of the controversy. (Committee on Children's Television Inc, (1983) 35 Cal.3d 197, 217 (superseded by statute on other grounds)). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: POLARIS DEVELOPMENT, LLC VS. ALPHA MEDIA GROUP, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants' Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings (Control Date)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, June 12, 2015, Line 10, DEFENDANTS ALPHA MEDIA GROUP, LLC, VDM GROUP, INC., VLAD MYASNYANKIN, GK GROUP, INC., YEVGENIY KHIZVER, TRACE CAPITAL HOLDINGS, INC., TIMOTHY EUN, AND GARY KAHN'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings. Granted as to all causes of action other than the eighth cause of action with twenty days leave to amend and as to the eighth cause of action for civil conspiracy without leave to amend. Civil conspiracy is not a cause of action. (Applied Equipment Corp. v. Litton Saudi Arabia Ltd. (1994) 7 Cal.4th 503, 510-11.) However, Plaintiff may amend the complaint to add civil conspiracy allegations. The complaint does not adequately plead facts establishing fraud or economic duress, which Plaintiff argues in opposition are the grounds for the rescission cause of action. Because the rescission cause of action has not been adequately pled, the breach of contract, intentional misrepresentation, negligent misrepresentation, breach of fiduciary duty, breach of oral contract, and oral book account causes of action are barred by the release in the Membership Interest Purchase Agreement ("MIPA"). (Complaint, Ex. B, MIPA, par. 5.) The second cause of action for economic duress does not adequately plead facts establishing that Plaintiff had no reasonable alternative but to agree to an unfavorable contract. (CrossTalk Productions, Inc. v. Jacobson (1998) 65 Cal.App.4th 631, 644.) The sixth cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty must be brought as a derivative action, as the complaint alleges harm to all shareholders of Alpha Media Group. (Jones v. H.F. Ahmanson & Co. (1969) 1 Cal.3d 93, 107.) The fifth cause of action for accounting does not adequately plead that an uncertain amount is owed to Plaintiff. To the extent that Plaintiff is asserting that it needs an accounting because of a breach of fiduciary duty, the cause of action must be brought derivatively. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL GALINDO et al VS. SECURITY TITLE INSURANCE CO. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Paul Galindo, April Campbell, Paula Buckley, John Adams, Belinda Campos' Motion To Compel Defendant U.A. Local 38 Pension Trust Funds Discovery Responses And Request For Monetary Sanctions As To Defendant U.A. Local 38 Pension Trust Fund And Defendants Counsels G. Kelley Reid, Jr. And David Wasserman
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday June 12, 2015, Line 4, Plaintiffs Paul Galindo, April Campbell, Paula Buckley, John Adams, Belinda Campos' Motion To Compel Defendant U.A. Local 38 Pension Trust Funds Discovery Responses And Request For Monetary Sanctions As To Defendant U.A. Local 38 Pension Trust Fund And Defendants Counsels G. Kelley Reid, Jr. And David Wasserman. Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties shall meet and confer prior to the hearing on all outstanding discovery requests in the present motion before the Court. Parties shall appear and apprise the Court of any progress, and shall be prepared to discuss each remaining outstanding discovery request. =(302/JPT)
